---
title: "Bifurcation analysis — hands on"
subtitle: "Session 3 · Dynamical perspective"
jupyter: python3
---

## Goals

- TODO: locate fixed points of a 2D mean-field model
- TODO: compute and visualise a 1D bifurcation diagram
- TODO: relate bifurcations to network-level phenomena

## Setup

```{python}
# TODO: imports
```

## Fixed points and stability

```{python}
# TODO: nullclines, Jacobian, eigenvalues
```

## 1D bifurcation diagram

```{python}
# TODO: sweep one parameter, track equilibria and limit cycles
```

## 2D bifurcation diagram (optional)

```{python}
# TODO: codim-2 sweep
```

## Exercises

- TODO: identify Hopf vs. saddle-node bifurcations
- TODO: connect the bifurcation type to a behavioural regime
